Title: Increased Apigenin in DNA-Edited Hexaploid Wheat Promoted Soil Bacterial Nitrogen Fixation and Improved Grain Yield Under Limiting Nitrogen Fertiliser

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Root-exuded apigenin promotes diazotrophic biofilm formation and associative nitrogen fixation, improving N nutrition and yield under low N
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No direct demonstrated effect on apigenin metabolism; included to enhance callus regeneration during transformation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GRF transcription factor fused to GIF cofactor chimera
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: NGS genotyping of edited loci; 16S metagenomic sequencing; qPCR nifH; LC-ESI-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter regeneration efficiency but not evaluated for metabolism
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Transformation aid for wheat genome editing, not a metabolic target
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GRF4-GIF1 chimera was included in the CRISPR construct to enhance wheat callus regeneration. 2. It was not tested as a causal gene for apigenin accumulation or nitrogen-fixation phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a core nutrient-metabolism gene in the Results; included only as an engineering helper in the construct.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Root-exuded apigenin induces diazotrophic bacterial biofilm formation, promoting biological nitrogen fixation and improving plant N status and grain yield under low N
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Apigenin catabolic branch to luteolin; flavonoid B-ring hydroxylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates apigenin at the 3' position of the B-ring to form luteolin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product accumulation by converting target product to another flavone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for apigenin accumulation in this study; simultaneous loss of chromosome 7 CYP75B4 copies associated with high-apigenin phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen limitation (30% N, 50% N) revealed biofilm, BNF and yield phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: CYP75B4 genes were not expressed at the callus stage; expression in other tissues inferred from functional relevance in leaves/roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Edited lines showed increased apigenin in leaves, roots and root exudates
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native edited alleles in wheat; 35S promoter used for Arabidopsis tt7 complementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multiplex CRISPR editing; deep sequencing genotyping; apigenin LC-ESI-MS/MS profiling; yeast microsome enzyme assay; Arabidopsis tt7 complementation; low-N phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: NGS amplicon deep sequencing of target loci; 16S metagenomic sequencing; qPCR nifH; LC-ESI-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function editing increased apigenin content in leaves, roots and root exudates, increased diazotroph-associated biofilms and nitrogen fixation, and improved grain yield under limiting N
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Editing of CYP75B4 alone showed desired phenotype; broader flavonoid shifts not fully quantified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for CRISPR editing to improve associative BNF and yield under reduced N fertilizer in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wheat chromosome 7 CYP75B4 homoeologs are functional F3'H enzymes in vitro and in vivo. 2. CRISPR lines with edits in all three chromosome 7 CYP75B4 genes accumulated more apigenin in leaves, roots and exudates. 3. Increased apigenin promoted diazotrophic biofilm formation, higher nifH abundance/BNF activity, and higher grain yield under low N.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chromosome 6B and 6D CYP75B4-like genes lacked detectable F3'H activity; simultaneous editing of 7A/7B/7D was most strongly associated with phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Apigenin in exudates promotes diazotrophic biofilm formation and biological nitrogen fixation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product accumulation by consuming apigenin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Functional for apigenin turnover, but not successfully edited in viable high-apigenin wheat lines; essentiality limits use
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen limitation used for Arabidopsis tt7 complementation and agronomic phenotype assessments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed at callus stage, unlike CYP75B4 genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: One line lacking CYP75B3 on chromosome 1D survived with severe growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for Arabidopsis tt7 complementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome enzyme assay; Arabidopsis tt7 complementation; expression analysis in callus; CRISPR editing attempts and regeneration outcomes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: NGS amplicon deep sequencing; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes functional F3'H activity, but editing/reduction was associated with regeneration failure or severe growth defects, so CYP75B3 editing should be avoided
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely essential for callus regeneration and vegetative growth in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important cautionary anti-target for editing; preserve CYP75B3 while targeting CYP75B4 to raise apigenin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Several wheat CYP75B3 homoeologs encode functional F3'H enzymes. 2. CYP75B3 genes are expressed at the callus stage. 3. Loss of CYP75B3 function was not recovered efficiently and one recovered line showed severe growth defects, indicating essential roles in regeneration/development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TraesCS1A02G442200 contained a premature stop codon and was excluded from further analysis; no efficient viable multi-CYP75B3 knockout lines were obtained.
